summary: Russian forces have secured the eastern part of Konstantinovka and are engaging Ukrainian positions on its outskirts. Ukrainian cities including Konotop, Poltava and Sumy have suffered multiple Shahed drone attacks, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukraine is evacuating industrial assets from Kramatorsk and reports a shortage of air‑defence missiles affecting ballistic missile interception rates.
key_points: Russian troops have fully established control over the eastern sector of Konstantinovka and reached its north‑eastern outskirts (Source: @tass_agency/380275).
Ukrainian forces launched guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/64845).
Shahed‑type drones attacked Konotop in Sumy region, killing a railway worker and injuring four others; part of the city lost gas supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
Multiple Shahed and ballistic missile alerts were reported over Poltava, including at least three separate incidents (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67550, @mon1tor_ua/67547, @mon1tor_ua/67551).
Ukrainian authorities are evacuating factories from Kramatorsk to Pereschyn in Zakarpattia as Russian forces approach (Source: @tass_agency/380284).
Ukrainian Air Force spokesman Yuriy Ignat noted a decline in ballistic missile down‑rate due to a shortage of air‑defence missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
A civilian bus was hit by a Ukrainian drone in Belgorod region, causing one death and two injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces have secured eastern Konstantinovka and are pressing on its north‑eastern outskirts. Ukrainian cities such as Konotop, Poltava and Sumy have endured repeated Shahed drone attacks, resulting in civilian deaths and infrastructure loss. Ukraine is relocating industrial capacity from Kramatorsk while facing a shortage of air‑defence missiles that reduces ballistic missile interception.

points: Konotop in Sumy region was struck by reactive Shahed drones; a railway employee was killed, four railway workers injured, gas supply disrupted, and residential damage reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).

Multiple Shahed alerts over Poltava: three separate incidents reported, including a ballistic missile and a Shahed drone, indicating sustained aerial pressure on the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67550, @mon1tor_ua/67547, @mon1tor_ua/67551).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, showing continued offensive air operations (Source: @kpszsu/64845).
Ukrainian authorities are evacuating factories from Kramatorsk to Pereschyn in Zakarpattia, moving 3,500 workers to western Ukraine as Russian forces near the city (Source: @tass_agency/380284).
Ukrainian Air Force reports a shortage of air‑defence missiles, leading to a reduced down‑rate of ballistic missiles compared with May and June (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
Russian troops have fully established control over the eastern part of Konstantinovka and have reached its north‑eastern outskirts (Source: @tass_agency/380275).
In the south‑west of Konstantinovka and at a metallurgical plant, Russian forces are destroying surrounded Ukrainian units (Source: @tass_agency/380276).
Russian reports indicate ongoing storming of urban areas in Konstantinovka with high‑intensity fighting (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces claim to have struck Ilyichevsk (Odessa region), Zaporizhzhia and Sumy with air strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced the restoration of Sevastopol's defence panorama will take about five years after recent attacks (Source: @tass_agency/380286).
Ukrainian small‑arms battles continue in Sumy district villages such as Bachevsk, Ivolzhanske, Pysarevka and Novaya Sich, indicating ongoing contest for control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
In Kharkiv direction, Russian Army Group "North" reports offensive actions toward Kazachya Lopan and fighting in Vovchansk sector villages Ohrimovka and Losevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces report storming urban areas in Konstantinovka with intense combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian drone attacks on Crimea and Sevastopol, including jet‑powered drones, have disrupted fuel deliveries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces claim to have hit a passenger bus on the Melitopol‑Velyka Belozerka route, causing injuries and power supply problems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
A reactive UAV was observed north of Chernihiv city, near the village of Horodnya, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/64864).
Reactive UAVs reported near Dobryanka in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/96234).
A reactive UAV from the Black Sea was heading toward Mykolaiv region (Source: @kpszsu/64850).
UAV activity reported over Sumy region, including fast‑moving targets and missile alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67560).
A reactive UAV was sighted in the Shostka district of Sumy, with assault troops engaged in small‑arms battles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Reactive UAV from the Black Sea moved southward toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/64858).
Ukrainian drone strike hit a passenger bus in Belgorod region, causing one death and two injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian drones attacked civilian ships Natra and Zirkon in the Azov Sea, resulting in five deaths (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).
Ukrainian forces struck the Afipsky Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Territory and residential buildings in Krasnodar (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces reported destruction of surrounded Ukrainian units at a metallurgical plant in Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/380276).
Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb attacks on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/64845).
Russian forces used guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Sevastopol, including jet‑powered models, affecting fuel logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian drone strike on a passenger bus in Belgorod region caused civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian UAVs targeted civilian ships in the Azov Sea, killing multiple civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).

key_developments: Russian troops have fully secured the eastern sector of Konstantinovka and advanced to its north‑eastern outskirts (Source: @tass_agency/380275).
Shahed drones struck Konotop, killing a railway worker and injuring four, while disrupting gas supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
Multiple Shahed and ballistic missile alerts over Poltava indicate sustained aerial attacks on the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67550, @mon1tor_ua/67547, @mon1tor_ua/67551).
Ukrainian authorities are evacuating factories from Kramatorsk to Pereschyn, moving 3,500 workers westward (Source: @tass_agency/380284).
Ukrainian Air Force reports a shortage of air‑defence missiles, reducing ballistic missile interception rates (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30847).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/64845).
Ukrainian drones attacked civilian vessels in the Azov Sea, causing five deaths (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating control in Konstantinovka, threatening nearby supply routes and applying pressure on the eastern front. Ukrainian cities face increasing Shahed drone activity, resulting in civilian casualties and infrastructure disruption. The shortage of air‑defence missiles hampers Ukraine's ability to counter ballistic missile threats, elevating the risk of further high‑impact strikes.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is relocating critical industrial assets from frontline zones, exemplified by the Kramatorsk factory evacuation, to preserve production capacity. International partners are discussing additional funding, though political disputes (e.g., Poland's demands) delay transfers. NATO and EU members continue to provide air‑defence aid, but supply constraints affect operational effectiveness.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of Black Sea‑origin drones targeting southern regions, while Chernihiv and Sumy report frequent reactive UAV sightings. Russian use of guided aviation bombs suggests a focus on disrupting Ukrainian logistics in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Continued civilian drone attacks in Belgorod and the Azov Sea indicate an expanding Ukrainian UAV operational envelope.
